observations.text: Both invariant violations identified with precision — BR-1 (exactly one Carrier per Shipment) and BR-2 (only Supervisor can reassign Carrier) are named, justified, and given testable assertions.
Acceptance criteria array is deliberately empty with clear explanation that the spec is not implementable as written — exactly the right call.
Escalations are exceptionally detailed, each with multiple resolution options clearly labeled as proposals (not decisions), preserving stakeholder authority.
Confidence at 30 with strong reasoning — correctly identifies structural conflicts, not mere ambiguities.
Edge cases are well-constructed, exposing real operational failures (lost freight liability, partial carrier availability, customer tracking confusion) rather than hypothetical corner cases.
Correctly identifies that 'sub-shipment' is a new concept not in the current domain model, treating its introduction as a third violation requiring domain modeling work.
Business impact analysis covers financial, operational, user, and risk dimensions with specific stakeholder groups and their concerns.
Option A recommendation (ShipmentGroup aggregate) is a sound domain modeling proposal that resolves contradictions without breaking existing invariants.
